intro: Pseudocalymma elegans, also known as the African dogbone tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its unique, elliptical-shaped wood. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues.
